title: penstemon cobaea var. purpureus
intro: Penstemon cobaea var. purpureus, also known as Purple Beardtongue, is a perennial flowering plant in the plantain family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, burns, and skin infections. The health benefits of Penstemon cobaea var. purpureus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system, making it a popular herb in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a carrier oil.
